trends.name: Global Trust Referendum on Innovation
trends.description: Societies globally conducting a de facto referendum on whether technological innovation — particularly AI — serves or harms public interests, with trust outcomes determining adoption trajectories.

signals.name: Innovation Trust Diverging by Market
signals.description: Edelman data revealing significant cross-market divergence in how much citizens trust innovation, with emerging markets more optimistic and developed markets more skeptical.

entries.filename: EMARKETER - The trends driving 'dupe' culture mainstream_CAIG.pdf

trends.name: Dupe Culture Mainstreaming
trends.description: Consumer embrace of affordable product alternatives ('dupes') crossing from niche behavior to mainstream — with store brand spending hitting $236.6B in 2023 and projected to surpass $250B in 2024.

trends.name: Private Label Premiumization
trends.description: Retailers elevating private label products with premium positioning and branding, blurring the line between store brands and national brands in consumer perception.

signals.name: Store Brand Spending Record in 2023
signals.description: Private label spending hitting all-time records as financial pressure drives consumers toward affordable alternatives, with sustained growth projected beyond 2024.

trends.name: Digital Ad Market Maturation
trends.description: US digital advertising entering a period of modest, predictable growth after years of pandemic-driven volatility — a mature market with growth decelerating toward 11% by 2027.

trends.name: Cookie Deprecation Final Chapter
trends.description: Third-party cookie elimination finally arriving after years of delays, forcing the entire advertising ecosystem to complete the transition to alternative identity and targeting infrastructure.

signals.name: CTV Measurement Fragmentation Persisting
signals.description: Connected TV advertising still hampered by measurement inconsistency across platforms, limiting advertiser confidence and slowing budget migration from linear TV.

trends.name: Automotive Emissions Technology Acceleration Since 1975
trends.description: EPA data showing continuous improvement in vehicle fuel economy and emissions technology over five decades — with electrification representing the steepest inflection in the historical trend.

signals.name: EV Technology Adoption Curve Steepening
signals.description: EPA vehicle technology data showing accelerating EV adoption across vehicle categories, with electric powertrains reaching meaningful market share for the first time in the manufacturer compliance data.

trends.name: Twin Transition Delivery Pressure
trends.description: European Union facing critical delivery test on its twin digital and green transitions — policy commitments requiring accelerated implementation in 2024 as political will wavers and complexity grows.

trends.name: Multipolarity Reshaping Global Order
trends.description: Geopolitical power distributing across multiple centers as US-EU-China relationships destabilize and emerging markets expand influence over global agenda-setting.

signals.name: India Emerging as Great Power Candidate
signals.description: India's economic trajectory and geopolitical positioning qualifying it as a genuine great power candidate in 2024, reshaping Indo-Pacific dynamics and global supply chain strategies.

signals.name: EU Automotive Sector at Inflection
signals.description: European automotive industry facing existential strategic decisions about electrification pace, Chinese competition, and industrial policy support — with major consequences for EU employment.

trends.name: Digital Infrastructure as Revenue Driver
trends.description: Leading businesses treating technology infrastructure investment as a direct revenue multiplier rather than a cost center — digital interconnection driving growth rather than supporting it.

trends.name: Edge and Ecosystem Integration
trends.description: Interconnected digital ecosystems at the edge becoming the architecture for business value creation — moving beyond centralized cloud toward distributed digital proximity infrastructure.

signals.name: Digital Economy Growth Measurable via Interconnection Bandwidth
signals.description: Interconnection bandwidth emerging as a leading indicator of digital economy health, with Equinix GXI data providing real-time visibility into enterprise technology investment patterns.

entries.filename: ESFERA - Risk in Focus, Ciberseguridad 2024 ES.pdf

trends.name: Cybersecurity and Talent as Top Enterprise Risks
trends.description: Cybersecurity and talent management emerging as co-equal top risks for internal auditors across European organizations — linking digital and human capital risks at the governance level.

signals.name: Third-Party Risk Escalating
signals.description: Third-party and supply chain risk elevating as a board-level concern, driven by interconnected digital dependencies and high-profile vendor failure incidents.

trends.name: Economic Fragmentation Hardening
trends.description: Global economy hardening toward fragmentation into competing trading blocs — a structural trend accelerating in 2024 as great power competition, tariffs, and industrial policy diverge.

trends.name: Geopolitical Swing States Rising
trends.description: Mid-tier geopolitical actors gaining outsized influence by positioning themselves as swing states between major power blocs — expanding their leverage over global trade, investment, and governance.

signals.name: 2024 US Election as Geostrategic Pivot
signals.description: US 2024 election identified by EY as a key geostrategic variable with major implications for trade policy, alliance structures, and global business operating environments.

trends.name: Media Power Redistribution
trends.description: Fundamental shift in who holds creative and editorial power in the media ecosystem — from traditional publishers toward AI systems, individual creators, and algorithmically empowered audiences.

trends.name: AI Agency in Media Creation
trends.description: AI transitioning from content creation tool to an entity with genuine agency in shaping the information landscape — not merely automating tasks but altering who controls narrative.

signals.name: Publishers Yielding Authority to AI Systems
signals.description: Traditional media publishers losing editorial authority to AI systems capable of generating content instantly and at scale, disrupting the gatekeeping function of legacy media.

entries.filename: EZRA EEMAN_Wayfinder Review Preview 23-24 Trends Report.pdf

trends.name: Future Shock Entering Mainstream Experience
trends.description: Alvin Toffler's 'future shock' concept — stress from rapid change outpacing adaptability — becoming a lived mass experience as exponential AI growth surpasses collective capacity to adjust.

signals.name: AI Adoption Hockey Stick Mirroring TikTok Growth
signals.description: AI application adoption curves matching TikTok's explosive growth trajectory — indicating a similar cultural and behavioral transformation is underway at comparable speed.
